title: Classic Spaghetti Bolognese
description: Boil 400g of spaghetti in salted water. Sauté diced onions, carrots, and celery in a pan with 500g minced beef. Add a tin of chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, and a splash of red wine. Simmer for 30 minutes. Serve over the cooked spaghetti.

title: Avocado Toast
description: Toast a slice of artisan bread. Mash a ripe avocado in a bowl with a pinch of salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Spread the mashed avocado evenly over the toasted bread. Top with an optional fried egg.

title: Creamy Tomato Soup
description: Sauté onions and garlic in a pot. Add 800g of canned crushed tomatoes, vegetable broth, and a dash of sugar. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes. Blend the soup until smooth, stir in 50ml of heavy cream, and season with salt and pepper.

title: Chicken Curry
description: Fry diced onions, garlic, and ginger until fragrant. Add 3 tablespoons of curry powder, diced chicken breast, and coconut milk. Simmer gently for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through. Serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Vegetable Stir Fry
description: Heat oil in a wok. Add sliced bell peppers, broccoli florets, and carrots. Stir-fry for 5 minutes. Toss in cooked noodles and 3 tablespoons of soy sauce. Stir continuously for another 2 minutes and serve immediately.

title: Banana Pancakes
description: Mash two ripe bananas in a mixing bowl. Whisk in 1 egg, 100ml of milk, and 100g of flour. Pour small ladles of batter into a hot, greased skillet. Cook until bubbles form, flip, and cook until golden brown on the other side.

title: Greek Salad
description: Chop cucumbers, tomatoes, red onion, and bell peppers. Add kalamata olives and a block of feta cheese. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle dried oregano, and season with salt and pepper. Toss gently to combine.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Sauté mushrooms in butter. Add arborio rice and toast slightly. Slowly add warm vegetable broth one cup at a time, stirring constantly until absorbed. Continue this process for 20 minutes until the rice is creamy. Stir in parmesan cheese.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Cook minced beef with taco seasoning in a pan. Warm up corn tortillas. Fill the tortillas with the seasoned beef, diced onions, chopped coriander, and a dollop of sour cream. Serve with lime wedges.

title: Chocolate Brownies
description: Melt 200g of dark chocolate with 200g butter. Stir in 300g sugar, 4 eggs, and 100g flour. Pour the batter into a lined baking tin. Bake at 180°C for 25 minutes. Let it cool before cutting into squares.

title: Lemon Garlic Salmon
description: Place salmon fillets on a baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil, and top with minced garlic, salt, pepper, and lemon slices. Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes until the fish flakes easily with a fork.

title: Garlic Butter Steak
description: Season a ribeye steak generously with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet for 4 minutes on each side. Add a large dollop of butter, crushed garlic, and rosemary to the pan. Baste the steak with the melted butter for the last minute before resting.

title: Caprese Salad
description: Slice ripe tomatoes and fresh mozzarella cheese. Arrange them alternately on a plate. Top with fresh basil leaves. Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and balsamic glaze.

title: Classic Macaroni and Cheese
description: Boil 400g macaroni. In a separate pan, melt butter, whisk in flour, and cook for 1 minute. Gradually add milk, stirring until thickened. Stir in cheddar cheese until melted. Combine the cheese sauce with the cooked macaroni and bake for 15 minutes.

title: Classic Caesar Salad
description: Chop romaine lettuce. Toss with croutons, parmesan cheese, and Caesar dressing. Top with grilled and sliced chicken breast.

title: Apple Pie
description: Line a pie dish with rolled pastry. Toss sliced apples with sugar, cinnamon, and a little flour. Pour into the pastry crust. Cover with a top crust, seal the edges, and cut air vents. Bake at 190°C for 50 minutes.

title: Vegetable Quiche
description: Press a pastry crust into a tart pan. Whisk 4 eggs with 100ml of cream, salt, and pepper. Add sautéed spinach, mushrooms, and diced cheese to the crust. Pour the egg mixture over the top. Bake at 180°C for 35 minutes.

title: French Toast
description: Whisk 2 eggs, 50ml milk, and a pinch of cinnamon in a flat dish. Dip slices of bread into the mixture until soaked. Fry in a buttered pan over medium heat until golden brown on both sides. Serve with maple syrup.

title: Fettuccine Alfredo
description: Cook fettuccine according to package directions. Melt butter and cream cheese in a saucepan. Whisk in heavy cream, garlic powder, and parmesan cheese. Toss the cooked pasta in the rich sauce and serve hot.

title: Spicy Tomato Shakshuka
description: Sauté onions, peppers, and garlic in a skillet. Add chopped tomatoes, cumin, and paprika, and simmer until thick. Make small wells in the sauce and crack eggs directly into them. Cover and cook on low for 8 minutes until whites are set.

title: Roasted Butternut Squash Soup
description: Roast diced butternut squash and onions at 200°C for 30 minutes. Transfer to a pot with vegetable broth. Simmer for 10 minutes. Blend until completely smooth. Stir in a splash of coconut cream before serving.

title: BBQ Pulled Pork
description: Place a pork shoulder in a slow cooker. Pour barbecue sauce, apple cider vinegar, and brown sugar over the meat. Cook on low for 8 hours. Remove the pork, shred it with two forks, and mix back into the cooking juices.

title: Garlic Bread
description: Mix softened butter with minced garlic and chopped parsley. Slice a baguette and spread the garlic butter mixture evenly on the cut sides. Bake at 200°C for 10 minutes until the edges are golden and crispy.

title: Lemon Drizzle Cake
description: Cream 200g butter with 200g sugar. Beat in 4 eggs, 200g self-raising flour, and lemon zest. Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes. While warm, poke holes in the cake and pour over a mixture of lemon juice and granulated sugar.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
description: Boil chopped carrots, celery, and chicken pieces in chicken broth for 20 minutes. Remove the chicken, shred the meat, and return it to the pot with a handful of noodles. Simmer until the noodles are soft. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Homemade Pizza
description: Roll out pizza dough. Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over the dough. Top with shredded mozzarella cheese, sliced pepperoni, and fresh basil. Bake at 220°C for 12-15 minutes until the crust is nicely browned.

title: Beef Stroganoff
description: Sear strips of beef and sliced mushrooms in a pan. Remove from the pan. In the same pan, sauté onions and stir in beef broth and sour cream to create a creamy sauce. Add the beef and mushrooms back in and warm gently.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Cream 150g butter with 150g sugar. Beat in 1 egg and 1 teaspoon of vanilla. Gradually fold in 200g flour, a pinch of salt, and 100g chocolate chips. Scoop small balls of dough onto a baking sheet. Bake at 180°C for 12 minutes.

title: Teriyaki Chicken
description: Cut chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces and cook in a pan until browned. In a separate bowl, mix soy sauce, honey, and minced garlic. Pour the sauce over the chicken and let it simmer until sticky and thickened.

title: Garlic Roasted Brussels Sprouts
description: Toss halved Brussels sprouts with olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet in a single layer. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until the edges are crispy and darkened.
